sub: Tension, your pace — always your call to skip.

1.name: Simmer
1.sub: Compliments, noticing, the pull you can't quite name yet.
q: What's the exact moment tonight you thought, 'okay, I'm paying attention now'?
Say one thing you find attractive about them that has nothing to do with how they look.
What's a 'type' you'd never admit to on a first date? Admit it now.
Finish this out loud: 'I noticed you before you noticed me when—'
What's the most attractive thing someone can do without even trying?
Tell them the exact detail of how they look tonight that you clocked first.
What's a compliment that does nothing for you — and one that gets you every time?
Name one thing they've said tonight that you're still thinking about.
What's something about them tonight that you didn't expect to find this attractive?
What's a compliment you'd believe instantly if they said it, no matter how over-the-top?
What's the most honest reason you said yes to tonight — and if you're still deciding, that's a fine answer too.
Say the compliment you'd give them if you knew for certain it wouldn't go to their head.
What's one thing about the way they laugh that you like more than you'd ever say out loud?
Tell them what you'd order for them if you had to guess — then say why that feels like a compliment.
What's a small, deliberate thing you did tonight hoping they'd notice? Did it work?
Name the exact thing about their voice that gets your attention, every single time.
What's something confident about them that you find more attractive than anything they could say?
Finish this out loud: 'Something I like about being near you is—'
What's a compliment you got once that you still think about? Would you want to hear it from them?
Tell them one thing you've wanted to say tonight but kept almost saying instead.
What's a look they gave you tonight you'd like to see again, right now?
Admit the exact second tonight the energy between you changed. Were you right about why?
What's one thing about tonight that already feels different from a normal night out?
Say, plainly, one reason you're glad it's them sitting across from you right now.

2.name: Heat
2.sub: Real want now, out loud — teasing and tension both fair game.
q: What's the physical tell that gives away when you're into someone — and are you doing it right now?
Say the thing you'd tell a friend about tonight that you'd never say straight to their face — then say it to their face.
Where has your mind actually gone in the last ten seconds? Say it, unedited.
What's the one thing keeping you from doing what you actually want to do right now?
Finish this out loud: 'The thing I'm trying not to think about right now is—'
Name the moment tonight the tension stopped being funny and started being real.
Describe, in one sentence, the exact kind of attention you're craving right now.
Describe the pull you're feeling right now without using the word 'want.'
What's the boldest true thing you could say right now that you're about to talk yourself out of? Don't.
Tell them the exact tone of voice that gets you every time — then use it on your next sentence.
What's a thought you've had about them tonight that you immediately filed under 'later'?
Say the compliment that would actually fluster you if they said it back to you right now.
What's the closest you've come tonight to just saying what you're thinking?
Name one thing about tonight that's making patience genuinely difficult.
What would you do with the next ten uninterrupted minutes, if there were no rules about it? Words only — paint the picture.
Finish this out loud: 'If you asked me to stay a little longer, I'd—'
What's the tension between you two right now, if you had to describe it as weather?
Tell them the one thing about their restraint tonight that's actually testing yours.
What's a version of tonight you've already imagined that hasn't happened yet?
Say the line you've been holding back all night — the one that would change the temperature in the room.
What's the difference between what you're saying right now and what you're actually thinking?
Name exactly where you'd want their attention next, if you're honest.
What's one thing you want badly enough tonight that it's a little embarrassing to admit?
Tell them, plainly: what do you want to happen between you two in the next hour?

3.name: Closer
3.sub: Slow, close, honest — desire with the volume up, never pressure.
q: You're in Closer now — what actually changed for you the second this tier started?
What's the truest thing you're thinking about them this second? Say it plainly.
Tell them one thing about wanting someone that you don't say until it's exactly like this.
What's something about yourself you feel more confident about specifically when you're with them tonight?
Finish this out loud: 'What I want, if I'm honest, is—' (Words only — say it, don't act on it yet.)
Name the difference between what you wanted an hour ago and what you want right now.
What's something about wanting someone that surprised you about yourself tonight?
Tell them what it does to you when they look at you like they just did.
What's the bravest true thing you haven't said yet tonight? Say it now — or say you're not ready. Both are fine.
Name one place tenderness and want feel exactly the same to you right now.
What's a moment from tonight you already know you'll replay tomorrow?
What's a version of yourself you only let them see? Describe it instead of naming it.
What's the difference between wanting to be touched and wanting to be seen? Which one is this, right now?
Say the thing you'd only say this close, this quiet, with no one else around.
What's one thing you're a little scared to want out loud? Say it anyway — or don't. Skipping counts here too.
Tell them exactly what 'closer' means to you right now, in your own words.
What's the last thought you had before this card — the one you weren't going to share?
Name the exact feeling in your chest right now. Not the word 'nervous' — the real one underneath it.
What's something you want tonight that has nothing to do with going any further, if you're honest?
Tell them the one thing about this exact moment you don't want to end yet.
What's the truest way to say 'I want you closer' without using those exact words? Try it.
Say what you're hoping happens after the last card — and know that 'nothing yet' is a complete answer.
What's one thing about wanting them that feels different tonight than it has before?
Tell them, plainly and without a joke to hide behind, why it's them you want closer tonight.

wild: Hold eye contact until one of you smiles first — no countdown, no closing your eyes to cheat.
Cup a hand to their ear and whisper one true thing about right now — something you wouldn't say at normal volume.
Rest your forehead against theirs, eyes open, for five slow breaths. Don't look away.
Press your palm flat against theirs, fingers spread, for ten seconds — no interlocking, just contact. Notice who's warmer.
Close the gap until you're sitting closer than feels strictly necessary — and stay there for the next card.
Take their hand and let it go quiet for ten seconds. Notice what that does.
Stand up, hold each other, and sway for thirty seconds with no music playing. First to hum loses.
Close your eyes. Let them trace a single word on your forearm with a fingertip — guess it before you open your eyes again.
Rest a hand over their heart for ten seconds. Say one word for what you notice.
Move in close enough to actually notice how they smell right now — and tell them, honestly, if you like it.
Kiss the back of their hand, slowly, like it's the only thing on the agenda tonight.
Match your breathing to theirs for six full breaths without telling them you're doing it — then tell them.
Hold your palm an inch from their cheek, close enough to feel the warmth, no closer, for five seconds.
Stand on opposite sides of the room. Walk toward each other at half your normal speed — no touching until you arrive.
Ask them a yes-or-no question using only your eyes and your face — no words. They answer the same way.
Write the truest compliment you've got across the back of your own hand, show it to them for three seconds, then wipe it clean.
final: Last card. Put the phone down after this one — everything else can wait thirty seconds.
Say one true thing about tonight that you know you'll still mean tomorrow morning.
Then close the distance however feels right to you both. That's the whole rest of the game.
